functions.description: Kill non-<Event> wrapper chunks: the <?xml ...?><Events> preamble the breaker emits as its first chunk (text before the first lookahead match), and any other chunk that isn't a real <Event> element.

Parse System + EventData out of the <Event> XML, map by EventID into ECS core fields, and capture log.file.path from Cribl's built-in 'source' field before clearing/overwriting it (source doubles as the ECS source.ip/source.port object for 4624/4625/4648). See cribl/winxml_to_ecs.js for the tested source of this exact function body (generated by gen_pipeline.js).

Enrich host.type/host.os.*/asset.owner from cribl/lookups/forge-assets.csv (generated by generate-assets-lookup.js from eforge/attack.yaml), matched on host.name (the FQDN the code function sets). host.hostname is already set by the code function and is not re-mapped here. host.os.type: windows is what makes the Elastic prebuilt Windows ruleset eligible on forge-*. Deliberately not user.name -- see generate-assets-lookup.js for why assigned_user goes to asset.owner instead.

Drop the now-redundant raw XML plus Cribl-internal bookkeeping fields. 'source' is not removed here: the code function already captured it into log.file.path and either cleared it or repurposed it as the ECS source.ip/source.port object -- removing it unconditionally here would delete that ECS data for 4624/4625/4648 events. cribl_pipe/cribl_breaker: the destination's systemFields is already [] and the forge-winxml breaker ruleset already has shouldMarkCriblBreaker:false, both empirically confirmed via _count/_field_caps to have no effect on the Filesystem Collector's collection-job code path (verified: cleared systemFields alone stopped cribl_pipe but not cribl_breaker; disabling shouldMarkCriblBreaker on the ruleset plus a full worker restart still didn't stop it) -- removing them here makes the repo the source of truth instead of depending on that undocumented behavior, same reasoning as owning the host object shape explicitly in the code function.
